\caption{Matched-loss quadrature ablation on target-adapted conditions. Dice-Exact is the exact level-set oracle; $M=2,8,32$ are midpoint rules. Methods are rows, datasets are columns, and cells retain CLIP-T and DL-T raw AURCs $\times100$ separately. Lower AURC is better as a ranking outcome, but a coarser rule can win accidentally; numerical fidelity is judged against Dice-Exact below and against the high-resolution boundary reference in the auxiliary study. AURC need not improve monotonically with $M$.}
\label{tab:quadrature-ablation}
{\scriptsize\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}%

\begin{table*}[t]

\caption{Per-image Dice midpoint fidelity to Dice-Exact on target-adapted conditions. For each statistic, cells give CLIP-T/DL-T in that order (a retained prefix identifies a lone condition in incomplete drafts). Error rows summarize $|C_{\mathrm{Dice},M}-C_{\mathrm{Dice},\mathrm{Exact}}|$; rank rows give Spearman $\rho$ and Kendall $\tau_b$. Exact floating-point equality is omitted because continuous knot and midpoint sums are not expected to coincide bit-for-bit. These diagnostics are descriptive and do not enter the four fixed contrasts.}
\label{tab:dice-quadrature-fidelity}
